_description: Example liquidity sweep scenarios — what each detection looks like in practice

scenarios.name: PDH Sweep → Rejected → Short Setup
scenarios.description: Price wicks above Previous Day High (sell-side liquidity), triggers stops, then snaps back below. Classic ICT/SMC short signal.
scenarios.trade_note: Wait for close below PDH. Short entry on next bar open or retest of PDH from below. SL above bar high (30358). Target ONL or next support.

scenarios.name: PDL Sweep → Rejected → Long Setup
scenarios.description: Price wicks below Previous Day Low (buy-side liquidity), triggers stops, then snaps back above. Classic long entry after stop hunt.
scenarios.trade_note: Wait for close above PDL. Long entry on next bar or retest of PDL from above. SL below bar low (29748). Target ONH or PDH.

scenarios.name: ONH Sweep → Rejected → Short Setup
scenarios.description: Price sweeps overnight high during RTH open, fails to hold, reverses. Indicates institutional selling above overnight range.
scenarios.trade_note: ONH rejection during opening hour = strong signal. Short below ONH. Target ONL. More reliable if PDH was also nearby (stacked resistance).

scenarios.name: ONL Sweep → Rejected → Long Setup
scenarios.description: Price dips below overnight low, sweeps buy-stops, then recovers. Common during first 30 minutes of RTH.
scenarios.trade_note: ONL reject + close back above ONL = long. Especially strong if aligning with daily bias BULLISH. Target ONH or higher.

scenarios.name: ORH Sweep → Accepted → Breakout Long
scenarios.description: Price breaks above Opening Range High and closes above it — NOT a reversal. This is a confirmed ORB breakout, not a stop hunt.
scenarios.trade_note: ACCEPTED = breakout confirmed. Long with SL below ORH. This is a momentum trade, not a reversal. Filter with internals: only trade if BULLISH or STRONG_BULLISH.

scenarios.name: ORL Sweep → Rejected → Long Setup
scenarios.description: Price briefly dips below Opening Range Low, sweeps stops, then immediately recovers above ORL. Common AM false breakdown.
scenarios.trade_note: ORL sweep+reject during first hour after OR forms. Long entry with SL below bar low (30354). Best when daily bias is BULLISH and market internals BULLISH/MIXED.

scenarios.name: Stacked Sweep — PDL + ONL Together
scenarios.description: Price sweeps BOTH PDL and ONL on the same bar — maximum stop concentration. The reversal from a stacked sweep is the highest quality long setup.
scenarios.trade_note: Two levels swept simultaneously = extremely high-quality reversal setup. Both rejections + LONG bias = strong long entry. Size up here if risk management allows.

scenarios.name: Weak Sweep — Below Minimum Threshold
scenarios.description: Price barely ticks above PDH by 0.5 points — not enough to constitute a real sweep. Filtered out by minSweepPts setting.

expectedOutput.reason: Wick of 0.5 pts is below minSweepPts threshold of 1.0 pts

scenarios.trade_note: Not every tick above a level is a sweep. The minSweepPts filter prevents noise from triggering false signals.
